en_title: Game Own-Ver: Why Owning Your Video Games Has Become More Important Than Ever
cn_title: 游戏所有权之争：为何拥有游戏愈发重要
subtitle: 数字时代的所有权危机
opus_summary: PlayStation停止生产游戏光盘，让数字游戏所有权问题更加紧迫。文章梳理DRM、下架风险与二手市场困境，并提出无DRM商店、数字二手交易和精品实体发行等替代方案。

paragraphs.en: Everyone has a foundational memory of borrowing a video game and discovering a whole new world. For me, it was Fallout. My friend Joe lent me an Xbox 360 copy of New Vegas: Ultimate Edition – the base game plus all the DLC on one handy disc – and over a weekend I became absorbed in Obsidian’s post-apocalyptic mojave. Since then, Fallout has become one of my favourite franchises. That memory serves as a good reminder that video gaming is an inherently shared experience, and borrowing games from one another is the purest form of that. It’s saying “I loved this experience, and I think you will too.”
paragraphs.cn: 每个人都有一段借来游戏、由此发现全新世界的基础记忆。对我而言，那款游戏是《辐射》。朋友Joe借给我一张XBOX 360版《辐射：新维加斯 终极版》光盘，里面包含本体和全部DLC；一个周末下来，我便沉浸在黑曜石打造的末日后莫哈韦荒原中。此后，《辐射》成为我最喜爱的系列之一。这段记忆提醒我们，电子游戏天生是一种共享体验，而互相借游戏是最纯粹的形式：它是在说「我喜欢这段体验，我觉得你也会喜欢」。

paragraphs.en: PlayStation’s decision to end physical disc production in January 2028 may as well have been a decision to cleave that shared experience in two.
paragraphs.cn: PlayStation决定从2028年1月起停止生产实体游戏光盘，几乎等同于把这种共享体验一分为二。

paragraphs.en: There is a kind of magic to physical ownership - the feeling of unwrapping a game for the first time on Christmas, flicking through the manual inside, discovering bonus goodies like a map or art cards. Many of those aspects have been eroded away by time and the push for cheaper production, but there’s one thing we’ve not lost quite yet: choice. You might keep that game for 10 years, or give it to a friend to play within 10 days; what happens to that game is up to you .
paragraphs.cn: 实体所有权有一种魔力：圣诞节第一次拆开游戏包装，翻阅盒内说明书，发现地图或艺术卡等赠品。随着时间流逝和生产成本不断压低，许多体验已经被侵蚀，但我们尚未失去一件事：选择。你可以把游戏保留10年，也可以在10天内交给朋友游玩；它之后会怎样，由你决定。

paragraphs.en: Whoever plays it next – whenever it’s played next – the beloved experience remains the same. A physical copy of a game is frozen in amber: they can never change. It’s the only way games like Grand Theft Auto 4, Alan Wake, and Tony Hawk’s Pro Skater 1 & 2 can maintain their original and often iconic music licenses – publishers can’t hijack them and remove the classic tracks because their deals expired.
paragraphs.cn: 无论下一个游玩者是谁、何时游玩，那份被喜爱的体验都保持不变。实体游戏就像凝固在琥珀中，永远不会改变。只有这种方式，才能让《侠盗猎车手4》《心灵杀手》和《托尼·霍克职业滑板1+2》等游戏保留原版且往往已成经典的授权音乐；发行商无法因为协议到期而夺走它们并删除经典曲目。

paragraphs.en: But above all of this, ownership matters because it’s an investment. It’s an investment players themselves make in their life, because they believe in the video game experience. Whether you’re 15 years old picking up extra shifts at your first job or 50 years old blocking out a weekend to enjoy a long-awaited release; the future of what happens with that video game should lie with the person who’s bought it and them alone. So why are our video games slipping out of our hands now more than ever? To understand that, we need to understand DRM.
paragraphs.cn: 但最重要的是，所有权之所以重要，是因为它是一项投资。玩家相信电子游戏体验，因此把自己的生活投入其中。无论你是15岁、在第一份工作中多上几班，还是50岁、专门空出一个周末享受期待已久的新作，这款游戏未来会怎样都应该只由购买者决定。那么，为什么我们的游戏如今比以往任何时候都更容易从手中溜走？要理解这一点，就必须先理解DRM。

paragraphs.en: There’s a misconception amongst players that once you’ve bought a digital game, you own it. You don’t. DRM, or Digital Rights Management, is the process of being granted a ‘license’ whenever we purchase content through digital storefronts like the PlayStation Store or Steam. What you have bought is effectively a long-term loan from the publisher & store to the player. As a result, it can be taken away from you at any time.
paragraphs.cn: 玩家普遍存在一个误解：购买数字游戏后，你就拥有了它。事实并非如此。DRM即数字版权管理，意味着我们通过PlayStation Store或Steam等数字商店购买内容时，获得的是一份「许可」。你实际买到的，是发行商和商店向玩家提供的长期借用权，因此它随时可能被收回。

paragraphs.en: The video game industry’s knotty entanglement with DRM has been ongoing for years, but it really ramped up in the 2010s, with publishers claiming the key purpose was to prevent piracy. Infamous incidents involving SimCity and Diablo 3 in the early 2010s, where ‘always-online’ DRM became a mandatory requirement for the first time, saw server malfunctions locking players out of their games entirely , whilst other times saw players log into games only to find their cloud-based saves had seemingly vanished into thin air. DRM games are like a penny on an invisible string - you pick something up that you think you’ll always have, only to have it suddenly snatched out of your hands with little choice but to accept what’s happened.
paragraphs.cn: 游戏行业与DRM的复杂纠葛已持续多年，并在2010年代明显加剧，发行商声称其主要目的是防止盗版。2010年代初，《模拟城市》和《暗黑破坏神3》等臭名昭著的事件首次把「全程联网」DRM设为强制要求；服务器故障会让玩家完全无法进入游戏，另一些时候，玩家登录后却发现云存档仿佛凭空消失。DRM游戏就像系在隐形细线上的硬币：你捡起一个以为永远属于自己的东西，它却会突然被从手中拽走，而你几乎只能接受现实。

paragraphs.en: Take Steam for example – when you load up a game via Valve’s launcher, a hidden process happens between the storefront and the publisher. It essentially asks “is this person allowed to access this from you?” Think of it as millions of digital threads linking up to one big chain. All it takes is for the publisher to yank on that (or, indeed, to be dragged away by the ever-present financial turbulence tearing the industry apart) and everyone’s access disappears. If you were dining out, and halfway through your meal, the chef came out and took your meal away halfway through eating it, you’d be outraged. Why shouldn’t it be the same for games? Oftentimes when games are delisted, it’s due to copyright licenses expiring or simply a desire on the publisher-side to free up server infrastructure, regardless of the impact that may have on their customers. The list of games now near-extinct due to digital delisting is extensive – Spec Ops: The Line, Resident Evil: Operation Raccoon City, Deadpool and Super Mario Maker to name a few. Now with physical discs on the chopping block – the only format those aforementioned games can currently be found in if you didn’t purchase them digitally during their available window – we should expect this list to balloon exponentially.
paragraphs.cn: 以Steam为例：当你通过Valve启动器运行游戏时，商店与发行商之间会发生一个隐藏过程，本质上是在询问「这个人是否获准从你这里访问该内容？」可以把它想象成数百万条数字丝线连接成一条大链。发行商只需拉动链条——或者被持续撕裂行业的金融动荡拖走——所有人的访问权便会消失。如果你在餐厅吃饭，吃到一半厨师出来把餐点端走，你一定会愤怒；游戏为何不该同理？游戏下架通常是因为版权许可到期，或发行商单纯希望释放服务器基础设施，而不顾对顾客的影响。因数字下架而濒临消失的游戏名单很长，包括《特殊行动：一线生机》《生化危机：浣熊市行动》《死侍》和《超级马力欧创作家》等。如今实体光盘也面临淘汰，而如果玩家没有在数字版销售期购买，上述游戏目前只能通过实体版找到，因此这份名单很可能会呈指数增长。

paragraphs.en: So why is PlayStation doing this? It has claimed it’s merely following consumer trends. Unfortunately, this isn’t entirely incorrect. The physical video game market has been in a marked decline – in May 2016, consumer spend was just below $6 billion; as of May this year, it’s barely passed $1.5 billion (although it’s worth noting that was a 3% growth, the first year-on-year growth since 2009). In PlayStation’s case specifically, the shift becomes more pronounced – in 2016, 27 of every 100 PlayStation games were purchased digitally; as of this year, it is now 78 out of 100. Hardware leakers have also reported the PS6’s manufacturing costs are pushing close to $1000, and with PlayStation boss Hideaki Nishino stating that it’s “not realistic” for Sony to front these pricey component increases, streamlining the PS6 as a singular digital-only console would assist in lowering prices.
paragraphs.cn: 那么PlayStation为什么这样做？它声称只是在顺应消费趋势。遗憾的是，这并非完全错误。实体游戏市场明显衰退：2016年5月消费者支出略低于60亿美元(约合人民币407亿元)，截至今年5月仅勉强超过15亿美元(约合人民币102亿元)，尽管值得注意的是，这仍同比增长3%，也是自2009年以来首次同比增长。对PlayStation而言，变化更明显：2016年每100款PlayStation游戏中有27款以数字形式购买，今年则已增至78款。硬件爆料者还称PS6制造成本逼近1000美元(约合人民币6780元)；PlayStation负责人西野秀明表示，让索尼承担昂贵零部件涨价「并不现实」，因此把PS6精简为单一的纯数字主机有助于降低售价。

paragraphs.en: PlayStation isn’t alone in this – Nintendo’s game-key cards for the Switch 2 and Xbox’s over-reliance on Game Pass reflect an industry-wide shift toward a digital-only future. And it’s not just the publishers either – developers must bear some responsibility for injuring physical’s future, too. Over 22% of IO Interactive’s 007 First Light European sales were physical , which is a significant statistic in and of itself and a clear sign of a viable physical market. Except IO Interactive shipped a physical disc containing only the first mission , requiring players to download a hefty day one patch to actually make the game they purchased playable. What value does a physical disc have if it becomes just a download key? At least it can be borrowed by a friend, but its use for any owner disintegrates the moment something happens to the servers that First Light is stored on.
paragraphs.cn: PlayStation并非唯一如此：任天堂为Switch 2推出的游戏钥匙卡，以及XBOX对Game Pass的过度依赖，都反映了整个行业向纯数字未来转变。责任也不只在发行商，开发者同样要为实体游戏的未来受损承担一部分责任。IO Interactive的《007 初露锋芒》欧洲销量中超过22%来自实体版，这个数字本身意义重大，也清楚证明实体市场仍然可行。然而IO Interactive发行的实体光盘只包含第一项任务，玩家必须下载体积庞大的首日补丁，才能真正游玩所购买的游戏。如果实体光盘只是下载密钥，它还有什么价值？至少可以借给朋友，但一旦存储《007 初露锋芒》的服务器出问题，光盘对任何持有者的用途都会随之消失。

paragraphs.en: The end of disc production doesn’t just threaten borrowing, but potentially the entire second-hand market ecosystem. Many players over the years, once bored of their collection, have found their way down to a local GameStop in the U.S. or CEX in the UK to trade in their games, and in the process, collect a few new ones while they’re in-store. Think of them as recycling plants for video games – as one player closes the book on The Witcher 3, another opens that person’s case for the first time. Second-hand stores have served as gateways for players to experience the extensive back catalog of past and present consoles alike, often at an alluring discount.
paragraphs.cn: 停止生产光盘威胁的不只是游戏借用，还可能波及整个二手市场生态。多年来，许多玩家厌倦收藏后，会去美国当地GameStop或英国CEX置换游戏，并在店内顺便带走几款新作品。可以把这些商店看作游戏回收站：一名玩家合上《巫师3》这本书时，另一名玩家第一次打开了他的游戏盒。二手商店一直是玩家接触新旧主机庞大游戏库的入口，而且通常提供诱人的折扣。

paragraphs.en: Publishers have tried to throttle the second-hand market in the past, with the likes of EA gating on-disc content via one-time keys accessible in brand-new copies only – a system that meant any second-hand copy of Battlefield 3, for instance, couldn’t access the online multiplayer modes. But some have decried PlayStation’s decision as ringing the ultimate death knell for the second-hand market. At least you could play some of EA’s games without an online pass. Without physical discs, there is no second-hand market at all.
paragraphs.cn: 发行商过去曾试图扼制二手市场，例如EA使用仅新游戏附带的一次性密钥限制光盘内容，这意味着《战地风云3》等游戏的任何二手版都无法进入在线多人模式。但有人谴责PlayStation的决定为二手市场敲响了最终丧钟。至少EA有些游戏仍可在没有在线通行证的情况下游玩；没有实体光盘，二手市场将彻底不存在。

paragraphs.en: But it doesn’t have to be that way. One pathway forward could be through the creation of a fully digital second-hand marketplace. Digital game storefront Good Old Games has championed itself as the first fully anti-DRM retailer - essentially, even if games get delisted from their storefront, it doesn’t matter. If you download one of its games, you can keep it on a hard drive and play it – no internet connection required – forever. .
paragraphs.cn: 但事情不必如此。一个前进方向，是建立完全数字化的二手市场。数字游戏商店Good Old Games自称首家彻底反DRM的零售商；本质上，即使游戏从其商店下架也无关紧要。只要下载过游戏，就能把它保存在硬盘中，不需要网络连接，永远游玩。

paragraphs.en: GOG’s approach could be evolved to give players even more autonomy. An online hub filled with individual user storefronts operated by players themselves – like a digital yard sale, players could browse one another’s wares, combing through for a good deal or a brand-new gem they’ve never heard of. Much like GOG’s extensive partnership program, or World of Books’s AuthorSHARE initiative, which donates a portion of all used book sales to the authors who originally penned them, publishers and developers could take a percentage of each sale in exchange for ensuring that ownership exchange of each digital title begins with the player handing it over and ends with the player purchasing it. No DRM middle-man in sight. This could also provide a second-life for pre-owned retailers like GameStop and CEX, thereby ensuring a key sub-section of the video game market continues to be served whilst, for the first time ever, actively incorporating the publishers and developers as financial participants. Idealistically, this could see a greater cash injection flowing into the industry, an alternative that would be much welcomed by many a developer over publishers’ hyper-focus on making their own live-service cash cow.
paragraphs.cn: GOG的做法还可进一步发展，给予玩家更多自主权。可以建立一个由玩家经营个人商店的在线中心，像数字庭院旧货销售一样，玩家浏览彼此商品，寻找好价或从未听说的新宝藏。类似GOG广泛的合作计划，或World of Books把部分二手书收入捐给原作者的AuthorSHARE项目，发行商与开发者可以从每笔交易中抽成，作为交换，确保每款数字作品的所有权转移始于玩家交出、终于另一名玩家购入，完全没有DRM中间人。这也能让GameStop和CEX等二手零售商获得第二次生命，确保游戏市场的关键细分领域继续得到服务，并首次让发行商和开发者主动成为经济参与者。理想情况下，这能为行业注入更多资金；相比发行商过度专注打造自家服务型游戏摇钱树，许多开发者会更欢迎这种选择。

paragraphs.en: It’s far from the only solution, though. One alternative direction would be for players to seek out third-party alternatives to get their discs, like boutique publishing houses. Limited Run Games , Fangamer and Super Rare Games are just some of the companies whose mission statement is clear: to take digital games out of your console, and put something real back into your hands. Limited Run Games initially began with humble indie game origins and have since expanded to work with the likes of Capcom and SEGA, proving the feasibility of these companies to scale their operations to meet traditional brick-and-mortar publishers’ expectations. If PlayStation’s lack of disclosure over cutting disc production to its triple-A publishing partners is true, it could well be those same partners have just as strong a desire to continue physical publishing as their fanbases - making for a potentially very bright future for these boutique publishing houses (provided a disc drive add-on is made available for the PS6 – something you’d hope would happen, considering Sony’s extensive disc-based back catalog and the continued production of physical media movies).
paragraphs.cn: 这远非唯一解决方案。另一个方向是玩家通过精品发行商等第三方渠道获得光盘。Limited Run Games、Fangamer和Super Rare Games等公司使命明确：把数字游戏从主机中带出来，把真实物品重新放进玩家手中。Limited Run Games最初从规模不大的独立游戏起步，后来已扩展至与卡普空和世嘉等公司合作，证明这类公司能够扩大业务，满足传统实体发行商的期望。如果PlayStation确实没有向三A级发行合作伙伴披露削减光盘产量，那么这些伙伴继续实体发行的愿望可能和粉丝一样强烈，精品发行商的未来或许十分光明——前提是PS6提供外接光驱；考虑到索尼庞大的光盘游戏旧目录及实体电影媒体仍在生产，人们当然希望如此。

paragraphs.en: PlayStation’s decision serves as a harbinger for a potentially dire future for gamers everywhere. It is entirely self-serving, and will have ripple effects throughout the industry that we can only begin to speculate on. The immense backlash on social media to PlayStation’s own social posts has become its own news story, and there’s always potential that such heavy negativity may cause publishing partners to hit pause on their PlayStation social marketing efforts. Gamers have every right to be angry – we’re being challenged on the future of our right to own the things we’ve paid for. Allowing this to pass unscathed would open the door to a myriad of worse decisions: things advertising during pause screens, and intrusive product placement throughout classic franchises. How we respond to this decision over the next year may define how the next decade of the video game industry is shaped. The official slogan of the PlayStation 5 is ‘Play Has No Limits’; if we don’t make a stand now, the PlayStation 6’s slogan might as well be ‘You’ll Play How We Want You to Play.’
paragraphs.cn: PlayStation的决定预示着全球玩家可能面临严峻未来。它完全出于自身利益，并将在行业中产生我们才刚开始揣测的连锁效应。PlayStation官方社交帖子遭遇的巨大反弹本身已成为新闻，强烈负面情绪也可能让发行合作伙伴暂停PlayStation社交营销。玩家完全有权愤怒，因为我们购买物品后拥有它们的未来权利正受到挑战。如果此事毫发无损地通过，就会为无数更糟决定打开大门，例如暂停画面播放广告、在经典系列中植入侵扰性产品。未来一年我们如何回应，或将决定游戏行业下一个十年的形态。PS5官方口号是「玩无极限」；如果现在不站出来，PS6的口号不妨改成「你得按我们想要的方式玩」。
